fresh_facts.claim: U.S. and Israeli forces conducted intensive airstrikes on Iranian missile production sites, IRGC infrastructure, and command centers across Tehran and multiple provinces between March 29-30, 2026, with over 11,000 total targets struck since operations began.

Israeli strikes targeted four key ballistic missile production facilities and 29 other Iranian military sites over the March 27-29 weekend, focusing on strategic, military, and nuclear-related infrastructure.
The USS Tripoli, carrying approximately 3,500 Marines for amphibious and air assault operations, deployed to the Middle East to heighten military readiness.
The Pentagon prepared contingency plans for limited ground operations including raids by Special Operations and infantry forces, short of full invasion, as reported on March 29, 2026.
U.S. and Israeli strikes targeted more than 150 Iranian vessels and expanded to strike Iran-aligned Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) in Iraq's Nineveh province, degrading proxy networks.

Israeli bombardment of Tehran targeted northern, eastern, western, and southeastern districts near Mehrabad Airport and Babayi Highway defense corridor, hitting IRGC facilities, ammunition depots, and logistics sites.

Iran's missile capabilities showed evidence of mounting degradation from sustained airstrikes, with Iranian responses shifting to asymmetric actions including drone strikes on opposition targets and economic facilities.
IRGC command centers, intelligence facilities, and mobile/fixed operational nodes were targeted across Tehran in late March 2026.

Iran's power shifted toward military-security structures with the appointment of Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Supreme National Security Council Secretary on March 28, 2026, centralizing crisis control and sidelining elected bodies.
Reports emerged on March 30, 2026, claiming Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed, with unclear succession arrangements fueling elite power struggles.
Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani and Ali Larijani were reported killed on March 17, 2026, further destabilizing the security succession.
Opposition figures described a 'turning point' on March 28, 2026, with momentum shifting to the people against a defensive regime amid military pressures.
Widespread internet blackouts were implemented across Iran as of March 28, 2026, as part of wartime control measures following extensive military strikes.
War-related strikes caused extensive losses to Iranian nuclear, military, and industrial sites across provinces including Tehran between March 26-30, 2026.
Strikes on key Iranian ballistic missile facilities disrupted Gulf shipping and the Strait of Hormuz, with global market fallout extending beyond the Middle East between March 26-30, 2026.
Thailand secured safe passage for its oil vessels through the Strait of Hormuz on March 28, 2026, amid shipping disruptions from regional conflict.

U.S. faces domestic pressure for diplomatic solutions amid low approval ratings and upcoming elections, while Iran's leadership avoids overt negotiations to prevent internal destabilization as of March 30, 2026.
U.S. demands on Iran include limits on missile programs, nuclear restrictions, proxy force reductions, and recognition of Israel, which Iranian officials have rejected.
No ceasefire progress has been achieved in the ongoing U.S.-Israel military campaign against Iran that began February 28, 2026, as of March 30.
U.S. and Israeli strikes expanded to Yemen and Iraq between March 26-28, 2026, in the 'Second Iran War' regional escalation.
Analysts warned of potential strategic miscalculations during the early phase of the renewed conflict beginning February 28, 2026.
The NCRI publicly advocated for democratic change as an alternative to continued war or appeasement as of March 28, 2026.
Reuters reporters filed dispatches from Iran on March 29, 2026, describing ongoing airstrikes, funeral ceremonies, and daily life amid active military conflict.

Intense multi-wave bombardment occurred in Tehran during late evening March 29 and early morning with explosions across wide areas, with no new strikes or confrontations reported precisely on March 30.

regime_fall.current_status: Over 11,000 combined US-Israeli strikes through March 30, 2026 have devastated Iranian nuclear, missile production, and IRGC infrastructure, with deployed amphibious forces and contingency ground operation plans signaling potential escalation. Succession uncertainty intensified by reports of Supreme Leader Khamenei's death, widespread internet blackouts, military-security dominated power shifts, and asymmetric Iranian responses amid mounting military degradation and economic disruption.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Reports on March 30, 2026 claimed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed, creating succession crisis and elite power struggles amid over 11,000 combined strikes and deployed US amphibious assault forces.
